local_names.romanized_local: Or Luak / Oh Luak / Orh Luah

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Orh Luak or vegan oyster omelette. 'Omelette food product' (FOODON_03315595) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Orh Luak. 'Pancakes and similar products' (A0CKP) is the closest defensible parent, as Orh Luak is a starch-and-egg-based pan-fried cake.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for vegan oyster omelette or Orh Luak.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Vegan Truffle Orh Luak as served at a Singaporean hawker-style restaurant, approximately 300 g, comprising the starch cake, fried tofu, straw mushrooms, and chilli sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Oyster omelette (Or Luak) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) Foodo; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (component cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for a vegan truffle variant of Orh Luak. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo's standard Orh Luak (oyster omelette) entry, adjusted to remove oyster contribution and substitute fried firm tofu and straw mushrooms per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Tofu, firm, prepared with nigari, USDA #16127; Mushrooms, straw, canned, USDA #11267). Truffle oil is present in small quantity and does not materially alter macros. Values represent a best-estimate composite for the vegan version. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweet potato starch-based dishes — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (sweet potato, boiled GI ~52) and published data on starch-based Asian pancakes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vegan Truffle Orh Luak. Orh Luak is primarily a sweet potato starch and tapioca starch cake. The University of Sydney GI Database reports sweet potato (boiled) at GI ~52 (low). Tapioca starch has a higher GI (~70), but in the mixed-starch, pan-fried preparation of Orh Luak the effective GI is moderated by fat (tofu, oil) and protein (tofu). A blended estimate of GI 52 is used, consistent with similar starch-based Asian savoury pancakes in the Sydney GI Database. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g; ~960 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 300 g serving of Vegan Truffle Orh Luak contributes approximately 960 mg sodium (~42% of the 2300 mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid adding extra chilli sauce or soy-based condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from sweet potato and tapioca starch with low-GI profile (GI ~52)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index (~52) and moderate fat and protein content from fried tofu help attenuate postprandial glucose rise, but the glycemic load per serving (~27 g) is substantial; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion and pair with non-starchy vegetables to manage blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with favourable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio; elevated sodium per serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Vegan Truffle Orh Luak contains no cholesterol and has a relatively favourable unsaturated fat profile from tofu and vegetable oil, but the high sodium content per serving (~960 mg) is a concern for cardiovascular disease management and portion control is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegan Truffle Orh Luak is a plant-based adaptation of the classic Singaporean hawker dish Orh Luak (oyster omelette), in which fresh oysters are replaced with fried firm tofu and straw mushrooms, and truffle oil is added for an aromatic, umami-rich flavour. The dish is made by pan-frying a batter of sweet potato starch and tapioca starch with egg (in the traditional version) or without egg in the fully vegan version, producing a crispy-edged, chewy-centred starch cake. The tofu is deep-fried to a golden exterior before being incorporated, contributing a firm, slightly spongy texture and plant-based protein. Straw mushrooms add an earthy, meaty bite. The dish is finished with a house-made chilli sauce that provides heat and acidity to balance the richness of the truffle oil. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~148 kcal/100 g), plant-based protein (~5.8 g/100 g), and a low glycemic index (~52) due to the fat and protein moderating starch digestion. Sodium is elevated (~320 mg/100 g) primarily from the chilli sauce and seasoning. The dish is fully vegan, dairy-free, and contains no cholesterol. It is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes at moderate concern due to its glycemic load per serving, and for those with hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium content. Clinically, portion control and avoidance of additional salty condiments are recommended. The dish is a popular modern hawker innovation in Singapore, reflecting the growing demand for plant-based versions of traditional Teochew and Hokkien street food.
common_user_queries: What is vegan oyster omelette made of?
Calories in vegan truffle orh luak
Is orh luak suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Vegan Truffle Orh Luak. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo standard Orh Luak with component substitution using USDA SR Legacy data for fried firm tofu and straw mushrooms. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using sweet potato starch as the primary glycemic driver. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Foodo standard Orh Luak adapted for vegan substitution (tofu replacing oysters, straw mushrooms added, no egg in fully vegan version). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for tofu and mushroom component data. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy component data. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully vegan dish containing no animal-derived ingredients. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully vegan dish; no animal-derived B12 sources present. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite and reliable RAE values for this specific preparation are not available. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for Orh Luak and is not expected to be significant in this dish. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source composite. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source composite. 9. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~52, low) based on sweet potato starch as the dominant glycemic carbohydrate, per University of Sydney GI Database. 10. The cuisine tag in the input JSON listed 'Global', 'Ramen', 'Pizza', 'Pasta' — these are field survey restaurant category tags and do not reflect the actual cuisine of this dish, which is Singaporean. Cuisine corrected to 'Singaporean'. 11. Halal status is marked false as the dish is served at a single restaurant and halal certification is not confirmed from the source data. 12. Gluten-free status is marked false as chilli sauce and starch sources may contain gluten-containing additives; not confirmed gluten-free.
